summary: Thorough analysis of esquery.js hot path. 168K esquery.matches() calls per verify, each ~20-50ns via V8 JIT cached matchers. Tried isNodeTypeOnly flag, child/compound fast negatives, dispatch simplification. All within noise or caused hidden-class deopt regressions. Esquery dispatch path ~15-20ms of ~52ms traverseSync. Existing selectorCache and thesis #1 pre-merged maps already eliminate most redundant work.

[v1-accepted, not merged to v2] Replace createIndexMap (19ms) with binary search over sorted tokens array. Also defer sortedMerge of tokensAndComments via lazy getter. Binary search uses O(log n) per lookup (~17 comparisons for 155k tokens) vs O(1) with pre-built hash map, but eliminates the 310k-entry hash map construction entirely. Mean of 3 runs: 352.06ms vs 374.47ms baseline (~22ms improvement). Fingerprint verified: 4944605e99e3, 63 messages.

Replaced Object.defineProperty with direct assignment in CodePathSegment constructor, added fast-path in flattenUnusedSegments for all-used segments. Improvement only ~5ms (below 10ms threshold). Code path analysis allocation overhead is not the primary bottleneck.

Replaced Object.keys().forEach with for-in loop for rule iteration, added fast path for listener wrappers with explicit positional args (a,b,c) instead of rest/spread. Mean of 3 runs: candidate 391.01ms vs baseline 390.63ms. Rest/spread overhead is negligible in V8 Node 22; the wrapper function call is not the bottleneck.

Fast-pathed the empty-directive case in apply-disable-directives after confirming tests/bench/large.js contains 0 eslint-disable/enable directives. 5 outer runs: candidate mean 232.60ms (223.91-237.36) vs main mean 231.31ms (222.57-236.78); fingerprint stayed 4944605e99e3 and tests/lib/linter/apply-disable-directives.js passed, but the change regressed slightly and stayed far below the 10ms threshold.

Tried to streamline flat config finalization by reusing the flat-config schema validator, skipping duplicate top-level validation for FlatConfigArray-finalized configs, and reducing empty-option allocations in Config. 5 outer runs: candidate mean 245.43ms (228.62-291.10) vs main mean 238.57ms (225.20-256.79); fingerprint stayed 4944605e99e3 and focused config/linter tests passed, but the change regressed and missed the 10ms threshold.

Exhaustive investigation of lazy scope tree construction and traversal optimizations. Tried: (1) plain object literals instead of VisitNodeStep/CallMethodStep - slower due to V8 class optimization, (2) inline traverseSyncDirect combining AST walk with selector dispatch - slower due to V8 preferring separated two-pass approach, (3) parent-setting in constructor + inline traversal - slower (two walks worse than one walk + array iteration), (4) push/pop ancestry instead of unshift/shift - negligible savings but new dispatch method caused deoptimization, (5) CodePathAnalyzer forwardCurrentToHead early bail-out - slower due to JIT deoptimization, (6) lean inline walk replacing Traverser - slower due to closure vs prototype method optimization. Phase breakdown: analyze=32ms (outside editable surface), traverse=25ms (CPA=22ms), traverseSync=45ms (dispatch=40ms), other=35ms (parse+rules). V8 JIT deeply optimizes existing code patterns; every perturbation causes deoptimization.

Fast-pathed common TokenStore token lookups and adjacent comment scans. 5 outer runs: candidate mean 291.94ms (251.79-338.79) vs main mean 293.74ms (251.34-344.34); targeted token-store and affected rule tests passed, but the result stayed within noise.

Tried to reduce SourceCode constructor allocation by lazily computing tokensAndComments, lines, and lineStartIndices instead of building them eagerly. 5 outer runs: candidate mean 229.16ms (220.16-236.85) vs main mean 229.01ms (219.10-241.64); fingerprint stayed 4944605e99e3 and SourceCode tests passed, but the result was effectively noise and far below the 10ms threshold.

Wrapper elimination: moved try-catch + ruleId attribution from per-rule closures in linter.js into callSync/callSyncSingle in SourceCodeVisitor. Fast path now passes raw listeners directly. 3 outer runs each: candidate mean 350.60ms (348.13-352.10) vs main mean 351.17ms (347.61-356.54). Fingerprint 4944605e99e3, 63 messages. Delta ~0.6ms, within noise. Combined with prior push/pop ancestry attempt (~1.5ms), the full thesis yields no measurable improvement. V8 already inlines small closures efficiently; moving try-catch into the callee doesn't reduce total work.

getScope() is called only 9,113 times total (not 195K as hypothesized), takes 2.23ms total. WeakMap cache already provides near-100% hit rate. Avg 0.24us per call. No optimization opportunity -- scope lookup is not a bottleneck.

Profiled #flatVerifyWithoutProcessors phase-by-phase. parseSync=67ms, traverse()=21ms, traverseSync=33ms account for 99% of verify time. Rule setup + config glue + directives = ~1ms total. No optimization opportunity in per-file overhead -- it's already minimal.

[v1-accepted, not merged to v2] Safe integer fast path in no-loss-of-precision: add Number.isSafeInteger() early return to skip all precision checks for integers exactly representable in IEEE 754. Also move regex patterns and prefix checks to module-level constants. This rule was the #1 bottleneck at 125ms (24.8% of verify time per TIMING=all profiling). Most numeric literals in the 19.5k-line benchmark are safe integers, so the fast path skips nearly all work. 3 outer runs each: candidate mean 321.20ms (320.31-321.70) vs main mean 352.25ms (351.30-353.63). ~31ms improvement. Fingerprint 4944605e99e3, 63 messages.

[v1-accepted, not merged to v2] Replace VisitNodeStep/CallMethodStep class instances with plain object literals in traverse(). Each of the 193K steps previously created TWO objects (options hash + class instance with field initializers); plain literals eliminate the options hash, constructor call, and unused type field. 3 outer runs each: candidate mean 303.75ms (303.02-304.87) vs main mean 322.05ms (320.65-323.33). ~18ms improvement. Fingerprint 4944605e99e3, 63 messages.

Set-to-Array conversion in no-useless-assignment + hoisted RegExpParser in no-useless-escape. REGRESSED ~21ms. V8 optimizes Set iteration better than Array indexOf+splice. TIMING=all inflates per-rule costs 3-5x due to ~3us measurement overhead per listener call; actual per-rule savings from micro-optimizations within individual rules are in the noise.

Flat-stack prescan optimization (avoid ~90K array pair allocations). Changed prescan from [[node,parent]] pairs to flat [node, parent, node, parent] stack. All 284 CPA tests pass, fingerprint correct. A/B test 5 runs: candidate 133.52ms vs baseline 132.20ms, delta -1.32ms. Combined with prior results showing ~0ms with nested-pair prescan. Core issue: prescan costs ~2.5ms but CPA operations for trivial functions are mostly no-ops that V8 JIT already optimizes to ~20ns each. 229/559 code paths (41%) bypassed but net effect is zero.

Inline traversal dispatch eliminates 193K step-object allocation but requires a separate assignParentProperties() walk (rules need node.parent during create()). Two walks + callback overhead offsets savings from skipping step array. Candidate median 307ms vs baseline 303ms across 4 interleaved benchmark sessions. MIN values (294 vs 301) hint at a small improvement masked by GC variance, but median shows no gain.

no-undef rule only registers for Program:exit, iterating globalScope.through (pre-built unresolved reference list). It does NOT fire per-Identifier node. The rule runs once at program end and iterates a small array. No fast-path opportunity exists -- the hypothesis was based on an incorrect assumption about rule behavior.

[v1-accepted, not merged to v2] Compile fast-check functions to bypass esquery.matches() calls for 3 common selector patterns: (1) all-identifier :matches() marked guaranteed match, (2) compound identifier+attribute checks compiled to direct property checks, (3) child identifier+field compiled to parent type+field ancestry check. Stored as _fc property on esquery AST root nodes. 3 interleaved server runs: candidate mean 278.93ms (276.52, 276.94, 283.32) vs baseline mean 306.20ms (306.83, 305.85, 305.91). ~27ms improvement. Fingerprint 4944605e99e3, 63 messages. All 761 linter tests pass.

SourceCode constructor is only 1.3ms total. No eager comment attachment exists -- comments are stored in a flat array and accessed lazily via binary search in TokenStore. Line splitting (regex scan + array construction) costs 0.85ms. sortedMerge already deferred by thesis #3. Object.freeze costs 0.08ms. getCommentsBefore called 167 times per verify, getCommentsAfter/Inside: 0 calls. No optimization opportunity -- hypothesis was incorrect.

Compile selector fast-checks to bypass 97.5% of esquery.matches() calls (168K to 4K). Three patterns: (1) compound selectors with identifier+simple attributes compiled to direct property checks (e.g. Literal[regex] to node.regex!==null), (2) all-identifier matches selectors marked guaranteed match, (3) child selectors with identifier+field compiled to parent type+field check. Biggest win: ForStatement>.test from no-constant-condition had nodeTypes:null (field returns null from analyzeSelector), checked against all 90,855 AST nodes with only 32 hits. A/B 7 runs: candidate 129.51ms vs baseline 134.72ms, delta 5.21ms. All 33,325 rule tests pass (same 2 pre-existing failures as main). Fingerprint 4944605e99e3.

Code analysis confirms config normalization is not a bottleneck. Thesis #23 profiled phase-by-phase and showed parseSync=67ms, traverse()=21ms, traverseSync=33ms account for 99% of verify time. Rule setup + config finalization + directives total under 1ms for all 57 rules. Even eliminating config normalization entirely would save under 1ms per verify call, far below 10ms threshold.

Lean recursive walk (no Traverser overhead) + separate setParentProperties + inline dispatch. Two-walk approach: median 304ms vs baseline 305ms (8 interleaved rounds). Single-walk version (parent inline, breaks 1 test) shows ~2-3ms median improvement, MINs consistently ~10ms lower (290 vs 301), but below 10ms tolerance on median. Node 22 V8 JIT handles step-array iteration as efficiently as inline recursive dispatch.

Thesis invalidated by thesis #45 (accepted, merged). Thesis #45 replaced 193K step objects with typed arrays (Uint8Array for step types, flat arrays for targets/args). There are no step objects to pool -- the step encoding uses primitive typed array entries, not heap-allocated objects. Object pooling has no target.

Combined leaf dispatch (skip ancestry push/pop for leaves), inline AST walk (eliminate Traverser class overhead), and forwardCurrentToHead short-circuit. Mean of 3 server runs: candidate 299.11ms, baseline 302.05ms. ~3ms improvement, below 10ms tolerance. V8 22 uses optimized deque for unshift/shift making ancestry O(1) in practice. Lazy ancestry from parent chain approach regressed 11ms due to repeated array allocation.

Map/Set segment-to-assignment index in verify() adds construction overhead that offsets O(1) lookup savings for small allAssignments arrays. Identifier listener (30K+ calls) is already minimal work per call. Total rule cost (~12ms server) barely exceeds 10ms tolerance, making it impossible to achieve meaningful improvement from this rule alone.

Thesis invalidated by thesis #45 (accepted, merged). Thesis #45 replaced 193K step objects with typed arrays (Uint8Array for step types, flat arrays for targets/args), eliminating the object allocation premise entirely. Generator-based iteration would add ~50-100ns overhead per yield across 193K steps (~10-20ms total), likely causing regression. The callback-based alternative (direction point 3) was already explored by thesis #31 (inline recursive dispatch) which showed 0ms improvement.

[v1-accepted, not merged to v2] Typed arrays for step encoding + inline walk + leaf dispatch combining + forwardCurrentToHead short-circuit. Mean of 3 server runs: candidate 288.69ms (range 287.69-289.38), baseline 304.12ms (range 301.61-306.84). Improvement: 15.43ms.

Thesis invalidated by thesis #33 (compiledCheck) which already bypasses 97.5% of esquery.matches() calls, reducing them from 168K to ~4K. The remaining 4K calls at ~20-50ns each = ~0.2ms total. Thesis #57 confirmed that further dispatch list optimization gives 0ms. Pre-sorting listeners by specificity has no target to optimize.

Skip exit-phase dispatch for node types with no exit listeners. Pre-compute exitTable and hasExitFallback before typed-array loop, check before dispatching exit selectors on both leaf and normal exit paths. Interleaved A/B 3 rounds: candidate 118.51/118.93/122.51 (mean 119.98) vs baseline 118.28/118.89/117.52 (mean 118.23). Fingerprint 4944605e99e3, 63 messages. Delta -1.75ms (regression within noise). V8 JIT handles empty-iteration efficiently; branch-check overhead offsets savings.

Profiled scope WeakMap overhead: getScope() called 9,113 times at avg 202ns per call = 1.84ms total. Node-property cache would save ~50ns/call = 0.46ms. Far below 10ms. Thesis #22 and #51 negative knowledge confirmed.

Only 1 of 89 registered selectors ends up in the anyType list (ForStatement > .test). All other 88 selectors already have nodeTypes set. The anyType selector is already handled by thesis #33 compiledCheck fast-path. Improving analyzeSelector to infer nodeTypes for child/field selectors would move this 1 selector to a type-specific list but not reduce dispatch work -- the compiledCheck already avoids esquery.matches() for it. No code changes needed: the problem is already solved.
Thesis invalidated by thesis #33 (merged). esquery.matches() at 0. Only 1 anyType selector. No path to 10ms.

Node-type-based CPA bypass: built Sets of 29 CPA-relevant node types and 15 CPA-relevant parent types. Nodes where neither node.type nor parent.type trigger any CPA switch case skip all CPA processing (preprocess, processCodePathToEnter/Exit, forwardCurrentToHead, postprocess). Measured ~6ms improvement (119.0 to 112.9ms, 3 runs each), but below the 10ms acceptance threshold. CPA total overhead is only 11.4ms across 90,855 nodes (~125ns/node). Previous theses #4 (ForkContext allocation ~5ms), #11 (early bail-out caused JIT deopt), #29 (prescan ~0ms) also failed. The optimization frontier for CPA is exhausted -- the overhead is spread too thinly across too many nodes for any single optimization to reach 10ms.

Split pre-merged lists into guaranteed (identifier) and needsCheck sub-arrays with pre-resolved function arrays from visitor. Eliminates per-selector type check and per-dispatch Map.get() lookup. 5 interleaved A/B rounds: candidate mean 117.84ms vs baseline 118.41ms, delta 0.57ms. V8 JIT already optimizes Map.get() on small maps to ~2ns and branch prediction is near-perfect for the identifier check. Pre-resolution doesn't help.

Three optimizations to no-useless-assignment: (1) parallel currentSegmentInfos array to avoid Set iteration in 30K-call Identifier listener, (2) cachedSegmentsSnapshot to avoid repeated [...currentSegments] spreads per assignment, (3) segment-to-assignment index to replace O(n*m) allAssignments.find/includes with O(1) lookup in verify(). Clean A/B on server (5 rounds, only no-useless-assignment.js differs): candidate mean 277.41ms vs baseline 277.16ms, delta -0.26ms. Rule total is ~12ms local; per-code-path assignment arrays are too small for algorithmic improvements to outweigh index construction overhead.

Profiled per-verify() breakdown: parseSync=67ms (57%, not editable), traverse()=18ms (15%, CPA-dominated), traverseSync=29ms (25%, well-optimized), setup=1.3ms (1%). The thesis targets setup cost (rule listener registration + selector compilation) which totals only 1.3ms. ESQueryHelper construction is ~1ms; selectorCache already persists parsed selectors across calls; compiledFastCheck is cached on selector root objects. SourceCode is re-created every verify() call because lastSourceCode is cleared for string inputs (linter.js:1363), but caching it would violate constraint #4. No path to 10ms improvement exists.

Pre-resolved listener function references from visitor Map at ESQueryHelper construction time, eliminating Map.get + array lookup in callSyncSingle on 100K dispatch calls. Also added callSync2 fast path for CPA events (12K calls) to avoid rest/spread. A/B 5 rounds: candidate mean 118.47ms vs baseline 119.89ms, delta ~1.4ms. Total dispatch mechanism overhead (Map.get + selector loop + listener resolution) measured at ~5ms. Even eliminating it entirely is below 10ms threshold.

CPA total overhead is 11.4ms across 90,855 nodes (~125ns/node per thesis #56 measurement). Cannot reach 10ms improvement from an 11.4ms budget without eliminating CPA entirely. Previous CPA theses: #4 (5ms from direct assignment), #29 (0ms from trivial function skip), #56 (6ms from node-type bypass). All individually below 10ms threshold. Even combining all known CPA optimizations (5+0+6=11ms theoretical max) would not stack cleanly due to overlapping changes. The optimization frontier for CPA is exhausted.

Code analysis shows ancestry unshift/shift is O(1) with V8 deque (thesis #39 confirmed). 11,904 callMethod steps have total dispatch overhead of ~0.6ms (spread) + ~0.12ms (array allocation). Even eliminating ALL callMethod overhead saves <1ms. GC pressure from non-editable parsing/scope allocations (80% of 127MB) cannot be reduced from editable code. The ancestry array never exceeds ~30 entries (max AST depth). No optimization path to 10ms.

CPA emit2 optimization: changed 11,712 two-arg CPA emit calls to store args as positional values (step type 4) instead of creating [arg1,arg2] arrays. Added callSync2 to SourceCodeVisitor to avoid rest/spread. Eliminates 11,712 short-lived array allocations and spread operations per verify(). A/B 7 rounds: candidate mean 119.89ms vs baseline 122.85ms, delta -2.96ms median. MIN delta -4.57ms. Real but bounded improvement - total CPA event overhead is ~4.4ms and we recover most of it, but absolute savings below 10ms threshold.

Thesis premise is incorrect. SourceCodeVisitor already batches all rules listeners for the same selector name. callSyncSingle(name, node) calls ALL registered listeners for that name in one dispatch. The overhead is per-unique-selector-name, not per-rule. With 89 unique selectors already merged into pre-merged lists, there is no redundant dispatch to eliminate. The batching proposed in this thesis is the current architecture.

Replaced binary search with interpolation search in token-store/utils.js search(). Profiling shows search() is called only 167 times per verify() for 0.027ms total (159ns avg). Even eliminating search entirely saves 0.027ms -- three orders of magnitude below 10ms threshold. Candidate 125.42ms vs baseline 124.25ms, within noise.

Architecture analysis shows this is not feasible. CPA events are emitted during traverse() inside analyzer.enterNode/leaveNode. They create callMethod steps that fire during traverseSync() interleaved with enter/exit events. Rules depend on this ordering (e.g., no-useless-assignment needs onCodePathSegmentStart before Identifier events). Moving CPA dispatch to traverse() would fire all CPA events before any enter/exit events, breaking event ordering. The only alternative (single-pass inline traversal) was already tried by theses #31 and #37 (0ms). Additionally, thesis #63 confirmed callMethod overhead is only ~0.7ms (11,904 calls at ~60ns each including spread and array allocation).
Pre-compiled dispatch functions per (nodeType,isExit) pair resolving listener refs at construction time. A/B shows ~0-2ms. Dispatch overhead ~5ms total, cannot reach 10ms.
Profiled comment API: getCommentsBefore called 167 times/verify, getCommentsAfter/Inside 0 times. Total time 0.065ms (386ns avg). Far below 10ms threshold. No optimization possible.

Code analysis of lib/languages/js/index.js parse() method shows all four parser options (loc, range, tokens, comment) are forced to true and are essential. tokens: needed by TokenStore for getTokenBefore/After used by many rules. comment: needed for disable directive processing. loc: needed for error location reporting. range: needed for range-based rule checks. None can be disabled without breaking rules. The parser itself (espree/acorn) is in node_modules and cannot be modified. Thesis #3 already eliminated the most expensive post-parse processing (createIndexMap, 22ms). No further parsing optimization is possible from the editable surface.

Traverser class is already eliminated from the hot path by thesis #45. The traverse() method uses an inline recursive walk() function (source-code.js:1195-1218). Traverser is only used in getNodeByRangeIndex() which is a non-hot utility function. Thesis is moot.
Comprehensive per-rule profiling: no-unused-vars ~7ms, no-useless-assignment ~5.5ms, all others <2ms. Total rule execution ~29ms. No single rule costs >=10ms, threshold unreachable.

Parent assignment is already inlined in the walk() function at source-code.js:1196 (node.parent = parent). No separate pass exists. Thesis #45's typed-array walk already includes inline parent setting. This thesis is moot.

getScope called 9113 times/verify at 190ns avg (WeakMap cache near-100% hit rate). Total 1.73ms. Node-property cache would save ~50ns/call = 0.46ms. Far below 10ms. Thesis #22 and #51 negative knowledge confirmed.

Combined enter+exit selector dispatch for leaf nodes into single dispatchLeaf() method with pre-merged list. Eliminates one Map.get + function call per ~45K leaf nodes. A/B shows ~1ms improvement. Far below 10ms.

Compile fast-check functions to bypass esquery.matches() for 3 common selector patterns: (1) all-identifier :matches() marked guaranteed match, (2) compound identifier+attribute compiled to direct property checks, (3) child identifier+field compiled to parent type+field ancestry check. Stored as compiledCheck on esquery AST root nodes (cached across files). Mean of 3 clean runs: candidate 300.86ms (298.37, 301.27, 302.95) vs baseline 312.79ms (310.11, 311.54, 316.73). ~12ms improvement. METRIC_A: 167.63 vs 177.96 (~10ms). METRIC_B: 3330.80 vs 3370.90 (~40ms). Fingerprint 4944605e99e3, 63 messages. 388 files, 0 errors, 0 warnings.

Deferred func-def detection in isUsedVariable, iterative scope walk in collectUnusedVariables, for-loop instead of .some() for refs. Interleaved A/B 3 rounds: candidate mean 660.93ms (657.99-664.26) vs baseline mean 662.01ms (659.64-666.44). Delta within noise. Rule Program:exit callback costs only ~4.5ms (not the ~11ms estimated by with/without measurement). Max savings capped at 4.5ms, below 5ms threshold.

Safe integer fast path in no-loss-of-precision: add Number.isSafeInteger() early return to skip all precision checks for safe integers. Module-level regex constants. 3 interleaved runs: candidate 632.77ms vs baseline 662.20ms. ~29ms improvement.
Lead-repaired attempt record (added missing branch field). Original data: 3 candidate runs mean 643.98ms, 3 baseline runs mean 661.09ms. METRIC_A improved ~17ms. METRIC_B neutral.

Lead-posted attempt record. Combined CPA node-type bypass (~6ms), positional CPA emit args (~3ms), callSyncSingle dispatch + plain object literals (~18ms). 3 benchmark runs: 261.86, 263.13, 275.20 (mean 266.73). Baseline: 299.50ms. Improvement: ~33ms. Fingerprint 4944605e99e3, 388 files, 0 errors.

Lazy createIndexMap with mutable wrapper object (survives Object.freeze shallow). Threshold=200 lookups before building hash map. Binary search fallback for initial lookups. METRIC_B regresses ~250-450ms raw because per-call overhead of _getIndexMap() across millions of token lookups outweighs savings from deferring hash map construction.

Skip createIndexMap for files with >2000 tokens. Binary search fallback for large files, hash map for small files. 3 interleaved rounds: candidate mean 310.41ms, baseline mean 323.51ms. ~13ms improvement. Correctness verified.

Compiled fast-checks at parse time stored on cached selector objects. 3 interleaved rounds: candidate mean 564.23ms, baseline mean 581.51ms. METRIC_A -17.42ms. METRIC_B +3.47ms (within tolerance). Correctness verified.
Pre-merge selector lists by node type at construction time. 3 interleaved A/B rounds: candidate 263.65ms vs baseline 269.14ms. ~5.5ms improvement.

Cached ESQueryHelper pre-computed selector data (preMergedEnter/Exit, anyType arrays) in single-slot module-level cache with name comparison. Cache hit correctly skips constructor work (parsing, bucketing, sorting, merging) for 387/388 files in METRIC_B. But constructor overhead is only ~50-100us per file (parse calls already cached via selectorCache, sort/merge on small arrays trivial). Total savings ~39ms raw METRIC_B = 1.6ms composite. Mean of 3 runs: METRIC 577.64 vs baseline 577.78 (-0.14ms, within noise).

Tested 5 approaches for compiling selector fast-checks: (1) compiled fast-check functions for compound+attr and child+field patterns ~1-3ms, (2) cached nodeTypeKey + split enter/exit dispatch ~0ms, (3) direct dispatch skipping matches() for simple identifier selectors ~1ms, (4) combined inlined dispatch + direct identifier ~1.2ms, (5) inlined flat dispatch ~0ms. 91.4% of eslint-recommended selectors are simple identifiers where esquery.matches() is already near-trivial (switch+comparison). The pre-merge optimization from thesis #78 already captures most dispatch gains. None reached the 5ms metric_tolerance threshold.

Ceiling test: disabling ancestry entirely saves ~21ms composite but causes 1 correctness error. Savings from esquery.matches doing less work with empty ancestry. V8 22 deque makes unshift/shift O(1) per thesis #39. Push/pop plus reversed adapter adds overhead without reducing real bottleneck (esquery ancestry traversal in node_modules).

Pre-resolving visitor function arrays at ESQueryHelper construction introduces correctness errors (ERRORS: 1-2 in Workload B). Isolation testing confirmed the bug is in the construction changes (changing enterSelectorsByNodeType/exitSelectorsByNodeType from instance properties to local const variables), not in the dispatch code. Even if the bug were fixed, prior evidence from theses #61 (~1.4ms) and #68 (~0-2ms) shows this optimization yields well below the 5ms threshold. The fundamental reason: callSyncSingle's Map.get is already very fast, and the ESQueryHelper is reconstructed per file anyway (parse is cached at module level). The pre-resolution adds complexity without meaningful savings.

Skip createIndexMap for files with >10K tokens+comments (uses binary search fallback via existing search() function). Defer sortedMerge of tokensAndComments to lazy getter since it's unused by eslint-recommended rules. Combined: METRIC_A improves ~7ms (createIndexMap skipped for 156K-token benchmark file), METRIC_B neutral. A/B test over 20 pairs: baseline 263.61, candidate 258.99, improvement ~4.6ms median, ~6.9ms mean. Consistent improvement in 18/20 runs.

Extracted report closure into createReportFunction factory outside forEach loop. Closures now capture only (reportObj, ruleId, severity, ruleMeta) instead of full loop scope. Modified: 269.11, 266.13 (mean 267.6). Baseline: 269.77, 272.44 (mean 271.1). Delta ~3.5ms is within noise. V8 Node 22+ efficiently handles closure allocation - the per-iteration closure has minimal overhead vs a factory function since both create new function objects. Prior evidence from thesis #18 confirms report() is called only 63 times in Workload A and 0 in Workload B, so the closure body optimization matters less than allocation cost, which is negligible at ~100ns per closure.

Tested removing Object.freeze from FileContext constructor and extend() method (108K calls in METRIC_B). Result: 275.07 vs baseline 276.56 -- delta 1.5ms within noise. Each extend() freeze operates on only ~3 own properties (id, options, report) so per-call cost is ~0.1-0.5us. Also tested removing SourceCode freeze -- caused ERRORS=1 correctness failure (a rule depends on frozen SourceCode throwing on write). V8 Node 22+ handles Object.freeze efficiently on small objects; the overhead is negligible.

sourceCode.traverse() already returns a cached flat array (not a generator). Replaced for...of with indexed for loop over the 193K steps array. Modified: 263.44, Baseline: 266.09 -- delta 2.6ms within noise. V8 TurboFan in Node 22+ already optimizes for...of on arrays to direct indexed access, so the iterator creation overhead is negligible. The thesis premise (generator output) is incorrect -- traverse() caches steps in this.#steps array after first call.

Replaced Map.get string dispatch in callSyncSingle with pre-indexed flat array access (callByIndex). Stored listener index on each parsed selector at ESQueryHelper construction. Modified: 268.72, 272.58 (mean 270.65). Baseline: 269.98, 273.42 (mean 271.70). Delta ~1ms within noise. V8 Node 22+ Map.get with string keys is extremely efficient -- hash is often cached internally, making the overhead vs array indexing negligible for ~270K-450K calls.

Deferred lines array to lazy class getter while keeping lineStartIndices eager. Lines getter triggers second regex scan on first access. Benchmark rules DO access .lines (via getLocFromIndex and getLines), so lazy getter always triggers, resulting in 2 regex scans instead of 1. METRIC_B: 4200 vs 3456 baseline (regression from double scan). Lines computation cannot be deferred independently because rules need it.

Polymorphic Map values (Function|Array) caused ERRORS=6 in METRIC_B (consistent across runs). Storing single listeners as bare functions and multi-listeners as arrays creates megamorphic IC sites in V8. The typeof check on every dispatch adds branch overhead, and the get() method returning [entry] for single functions breaks stable reference expectations. V8 deoptimizes the entire dispatch path due to polymorphic map values. The monomorphic array-always pattern is V8-optimal.

Cache approach fundamentally broken: configuredRules is created via Object.assign({}, config.rules, mergedInlineConfig.rules) per file in _verifyWithFlatConfigArray (linter.js:1279-1283). This creates a NEW object every time, so any WeakMap/identity-based cache on configuredRules never hits. Even if fixed to cache on config.rules identity, the per-file overhead (severity parsing, ruleMapper lookup, options computation) is ~28-84us per file -- total ~11-33ms raw METRIC_B / 25 = ~0.4-1.3ms composite, well below 5ms threshold.
